title: What is SQL Injection?
summary: What is SQL Injection, the attack mechanism (' OR 1=1 --), the consequences, and two effective ways to prevent it: Prepared Statements and Stored Procedures.
content_html:
What is SQL Injection?

SQL Injection is an attack technique in which the attacker inserts malicious SQL code into the application's input, causing the database to execute unwanted commands.

This is one of the most common and dangerous web security vulnerabilities (OWASP Top 10 for many years in a row).

1. Attack mechanism

Suppose the application builds a login query as follows:

String query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '" 
              + username + "' AND password = '" + password + "'";

Normal case (username = 'jane', password = 'pass123')

SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = 'jane' AND password = 'pass123'

Valid query, returns jane's user if the password is correct.

Attack case (username = ' or 1=1 --, password = anything)

Query sau khi inject: 
SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '' or 1=1 --' AND password = 'anything'

Mark -- is a comment in SQL → the following part is ignored.
1=1 always TRUE → WHERE condition is always true → returned ALL users!

Common SQL Injection payloads

PayloadEffects
' OR '1'='1Condition is always TRUE
' OR 1=1 --Comment the rest
'; DROP TABLE users; --Delete users table!
' UNION SELECT username, password FROM admin --Get data from another table

2. Consequences of SQL Injection

  • Bypass authentication: Login without password
  • Data theft: Steal the entire database
  • Data destruction: Delete table, schema
  • Privilege escalation: Escalate admin rights
  • Remote code execution: In some configurations, runs a shell command

3. How to prevent — Prepared Statements

Prepared Statements (also known as Parameterized Queries) completely separates SQL code and user input data. MySQL server processes the SQL first, the data is passed in later as parameters — never interpreted as SQL code.

Python (mysql-connector)

cursor = db.cursor()

query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = %s AND password = SHA2(%s, 256)"
cursor.execute(query, (username_input, password_input))

user = cursor.fetchone()

Java (JDBC)

String sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ? AND password = SHA2(?, 256)";
PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement(sql);
stmt.setString(1, usernameInput);
stmt.setString(2, passwordInput);
ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery();

PHP (PDO)

$stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = :user AND password = SHA2(:pass, 256)");
$stmt->execute([':user' => $username, ':pass' => $password]);
$user = $stmt->fetch();

Whatever the attacker enters ' OR 1=1 -- enter the username, the server will look for which username has the correct value ' OR 1=1 -- (include apostrophe) — no such user → can't log in.

4. How to prevent — Stored Procedures

Stored Procedure is the SQL code stored in the database, receiving safe parameters:

DELIMITER //
CREATE PROCEDURE GetUser(IN p_username VARCHAR(100), IN p_password VARCHAR(50))
BEGIN
    SELECT id, name FROM users
    WHERE username = p_username
    AND password = SHA2(p_password, 256);
END //
DELIMITER ;

CALL GetUser('jane', 'password123');

Input is passed as parameter, not embedded in SQL string → safe against injection.

5. Additional measures

  • Input validation: Check input format (email must have @, ID must be number)
  • Whitelist validation: Only allowed characters are accepted
  • Least privilege: Application user only needs to SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE, no need to DROP
  • WAF (Web Application Firewall): Filter requests before entering the server
  • Error handling: Do not display SQL errors to the user — hackers can read the database structure from the error message
